semanticCore: Inclination, bias, swerving, deviation, cavalry charge, or attack
quranicRelevance: Inclining away from justice (Meeleh - 'wa yameeloona maylatan wahidatan'), and inclining towards one wife over others ('fala tameeloo kulla al-mayli').

keyDerivations.meaning: To incline, lean, swerve, deviate, tilt, or turn aside
Inclination, bias, swerve, single sweep, or sudden charge/assault

figurativeOrRareUsages: فَيَمِيلُونَ عَلَيْكُمْ مَيْلَةً وَاحِدَةً (4:102): So they might assault/charge you in a single sweep/charge (tactical strike during prayer).
فَلَا تَمِيلُوا كُلَّ الْمَيْلِ فَتَذَرُوهَا كَالْمُعَلَّقَةِ: So do not incline with complete bias (leaving one wife suspended).
discardedNoise: The spatial unit of a mile (ميل) in distance calculation.
summaryEn: The root represents leaning and bias, warning against marital unfairness (complete Mayl) and describing the sudden tactical charge (Meylah) of battlefield enemies.
summaryAr: يمثل هذا الجذر الانحراف والميل، محذراً من الميل والجور الزوجي (فلا تميلوا كل الميل)، ومصوّراً انقضاض العدو العسكري المباغت (ميلة واحدة).
rawText: أَمْيَلُ Swaying on horseback: see an ex. of its pl. مِيلٌ in a verse cited voce أَشْعَلَ. === عِمَّةٌ مَيْلَآءُ: see قَفْدَآءُ. استمالهُ 10 , and استمال بِقَلْبِهِ, (S, K,) He inclined him, and his heart. (K.) === اِسْتَمَالَهُ He attracted him to himself; or sought to make him incline. (MA.) === استمال is a quasi-pass. of 🡻مَيَّلَهُ. (K, * TA.) امالةُ الأَلِفِ The inclining of the sound of ا when quiescent, after fet-hah, towards the sound of ى; so that the fet-hah, with that ا, composes a sound the same as that of the long “ e ” in the English word “ there. ” This is accordant with present usage; and I have not found any learned Arab who asserts otherwise. See also نَابٌ, and حَجَّاجٌ, and مَشُوبٌ. تَمَايَلَ فِى مِشْيَتِهِ 6 [He affected an inclining of his body, or a bending, or he inclined his body, or bent, from side to side, in his gait; a meaning well known, and still common]; (S;) syn. تَثَنَّى. (Har, p. 269.) === See تَزَايُغٌ. === تَمَايَلَتْ فِى مِشْيَتِهَا and 🡻تَمَيَّلَتْ signify the same. (TA.) === تَمَايَلَ إِلَى الشَّىْءِ; and عَنْ طَرِيقِهِ: i. q. تَجَانَفَ [He affected a deviation, or purposely deviated from his course, &c.] (TA in art. جنف.) تَمَيَّلَ 5 See 6. === تَمَيَّلَ بِالقَوْلِ He vacillated in the saying: see تَرَجَّحَ. مَالَ 1 [He, or it, inclined, leant, bent, propended, tended, declined, deviated, or deflected.] === مَالَ مَعَهُ and 🡻مَايَلَهُ He conformed with, and assisted, or aided, him. (TA.) === مَالَ إِلَيْهِ He loved him. (TA.) === He wronged him. (TA.) He was, or became, inimical to him. === مَالَتِ الدَّابَّةُ مِنْ رِجْلِهَا (K, art. غمز,) i. q. ظَلَعَتْ [It limped]. (TA.) مَايَلَهُ 3 He inclined towards him reciprocally: and مَايَلَا they two inclined each towards the other. (TK, art. هود.) See also مَالَ مَعَهُ in 1. مَيَلٌ A natural wryness. (S.) مَيَّالٌ [i. q. مُتَمَايِلٌ, Inclining much]. (A, art. فيد.) See سَيَّالٌ. مَيَّلَ بَيْنَ شَيْئَيْنِ 2 He wavered, or vacillated, between two things. (S, MA.) See 10. مَيْلٌ Inclination; leaning; bent; propensity; tendency. مِيلٌ as used by the Arabs, [A mile:] The distance to which the eye reaches along land: accord. to the ancient astronomers, three thousand cubits: accord to the moderns, four thousand cubits: but the difference is merely verbal; for they agree that its extent is ninety-six thousand digits; [about 5166 English feet;] each digit being the measure of six barley-corns, each placed with its belly next to another; but the ancients say that the cubit is thirty-two digits; which makes the mile three thousand cubits. (Msb, which see for more.) See also مُطْلِبٌ === ميِلٌ i. q. مُلْمُولٌ, [A style]. (K.) مِيلَانِ (?) of a مَحَالَة of a well: see ثِنَايَةٌ.
